transcript: —I have the copy Porcupine someone was looking for.
“Porcupine”? Hang on one second. I don’t think it was me, so one sec.
You fuckin’ put me on hold when you’re at work.
Oh! Okay. Did you just call me couple minutes ago?—
Alright, Frankenstein, you want. Yes, sir. How can I help you?
I did not call you; I just opened up the phone to talk to you and you are there.
—But, ah, we’re in the book—we are a bookshop and we do have copies of Frankenstein.
Hi. We did not have Porcupine.
I—I thought it w—I thought it was Pork Pie.
I don’t know. You told me “Porcupine”—
Okay, did you already speak to someone here?
Okay, ‘cause I—I was wondering if you might’ve called Brian earlier and he might’ve told you something different—
—I don’t know who Brian is, sorry—
—Okay. So, what is the name of the book that you’re looking for, again?
Right, but, I’m asking you what is the book that you are looking for.
You told me to look for Porcupine book in the children’s section.
I thought it was Pork Pie. So you’re looking for porcupine books in general? We don’t have a children’s section, to start with. We do not have a—
—Hi, I’m c—I am a bookstore. I looked in our kid’s section; we did not have a book called Porcupine.
Okay, so you are looking for that book.
—So you calling back to let me know that—
—I didn’t call; I just put you on hold to look on the shelf.
I’m not—I don’t—not showing that particular title. Ah, if you have the author’s name, that would be very helpful, but just the title “Concubine”, nothing is coming up.
“Concubine”? I thought you were saying “Porcupine”! There’s the issue! [laughs]
What’s the—is it Concubine or Porcupine? What’s the title?
Of w—of which book? I’m sorry, I’m not following.
Okay, so I—I didn’t just speak with you; I just spoke to a gentleman and he was looking for a book and he said the title is “Concubine”.
I’m sorry, what—are—are you calling from an agency?
I—I’m sorry, I—I’m not calling; I think we received a call.
Okay, I’m not sure. But okay. I’m—I’m gonna hang up, because we do not have the book in stock. I’m sorry.
I’m sorry, I didn’t find anything like that.
—Uh, were you looking for The Porcupine?
You know—you know what? I think we somehow got into a double phone call. I don’t know.
Um, did you find it, by any chance? I can’t find it—
—No, I couldn’t—I—I wasn’t sure of the book, but something “Poke In The Eye” or something like that.
“Porcupine”! Okay. And I heard “Poke In The Eye” [laughs]
—Alright. Yeah, I’m—I’m not even finding it anyways.
Um, was there a subtitle at all, do you know? A lot of books are showing up with the title of “Porcupine”.
You sure it’s The Porcupine or “The Concubine”? [chuckles]
Oh, “Concubine”! Oh, my goodness! I heard “Porcupine”. I am so sorry!
I don’t know what the sound interference is that’s happening.
Uh, you said it was by Nora Lott?
Um, I’m seeing one by Nora Lott and I’m just checking if that’s the author.
—Um, yeah! If you don’t mind holding again, I can go double check—
—Also, there’s some kind of weird sound interference. Hello?
—if you don’t mind holding for just a moment, I can double check and see if we have The Concubine by Lott—Lott—
There’s a lot of sound ir—interference.
Okay, I was not able to find that book. Okay?—
—Calvin Klein? You did not get it here?
That’s what he told me the name of the book was.
In the back of the store? Calvin Klein? Uh, no—I couldn’t—I was looking. I thought he said “Porcupine”. [exhales]
Okay, no, we don’t have that book.
Oh! So yo don’t know what he’s asking for.
It—is is it Calvin Klein or is it Porcupine? What—what is the title? I mean, I don’t know—I—what—what is he asking for?—
—The name of the book was “Calvin Klein” and you got it here, from our store. He doesn’t know the author—
—and it’s about the designer—and I said, “We don’t—“
—I know, and I—I looked—and I just looked in the “Biography” area—
—and then I also looked in the “Fashion” area, and it’s not in either place.
Are you looking for the book, Porcupine?
—[faintly] I’m so sorry. Can you repeat that?—
—Okay, and—and you—you say I had a number of copies of it?
No, we don’t—so you’re looking for a book titled Porcupine, or is it a different name?
Well, he called me and I—he’s—I—I thought he said he was looking for Ernest Borgnine.
So are you looking for the autobiography?
[unclear] Talented. This is Ricardo. Good morning!
Is—is this—is this is a joke, this call?
No! Just gimme a second; I’m gonna put you on hold
Yeah, sir, I’m havin’ a hard time locating; I don’t remember this title. Um, is this called “Porcupine”?
Okay. Do you have any more information about the particular book? Is it about porcupines. Without an author, I’m not comin’ up with…
[Russel, an employee of Arcadian Bookshop, is connected]
—Well, I put someone on hold who I thought was looking for a book titled “Porcupine”. But I could’ve misunderstood that. So—
—Oh, “Porcupines”! I didn’t hear that. No, I don’t—I didn’t—I didn’t put that on hold over here. So, you got—got the wrong shop.
Okay, wait. Who am I—who am I talking to? This is Reina at Blue Cypress Books and I just put somebody on hold who called me and asked for a book—
—Oh, well you’re talking to Russel at Arcadian.
—That is so weird! Because I literally—someone called me looking for that book, and I said—
—“Hold on a minute; let me put you on hold”!—
—and then, all of a sudden, I’m talking to you; it’s destiny—
—and there you are! That is so weird!—
Okay, um, the only book we have is, ah, a title called, “Sparkling Wine Anytime”—
Hi! Um, I was just talking to you about the porcupine book?
“The Porcupine Book”! Gimme one moment, please.
Yeah! Um, I’m sorry, I was talking to a gentleman on this line; he was looking for a book called “Sparkling Wine”?
Oh! Okay, um, but did you ask about a porcupine book? A book called “Por—“
—Oh, I’m so sorry. Were you calling about a book called “Porcupine”?
—Oh, wait. What am I looking for? They’re telling me it’s called, “Poor Carmine”.
—No, I don’t think “Poor Carmine” is a book.
I’m not finding a book called “Porcupine” either! [snickers] Wait, I don’t understand how you ended up on the other end of the line, though—
—I don’t understand what you’re talking about—

—This has gone on long enough. I have to hang up. I have work to do, okay? Yeah, I have an inventory management system that allows me to look up anything we have. And it’s not showing anything up under just plain “Porcupine”.
[laughs] Uh, okay, I—I think the person meant to call you, I believe; I think they were looking for a book called “Turpentine”.
—Yeah, what’s the title of the book?
Oh! He told me, “Chorus Line”! Oh! [laughs]
Well anyway, um, we’ll let him unravel this one! [laughs]—
Hi, this is Wise Blood. You called about a book called “Porcupine”?
No, not “Porcupine”. The young man said that you got a book he called “Turpentine”. I don’t remember a book entitled “Turpentine”.
So I—I had somebody call us at Wise Blood asking for a book called “Porcupine”. But I don’t have anything called “Porcupine”
Yeah, I don’t have anything called “Turpentine”.
Uh, somebody else called me. I don’t know what you’re talkin’ about. I’m a—I’m a bookstore—
—I know you don’t what I’m talkin’ ‘bout. I don’t know what you’re talkin’ about! [laughs]
Hi! Were you just looking for a book called “Porcupine”?
[laughs] No, I just had a guy call and ask for “Poor Carmine”.
I’m the book shop in Lakewood!
Actually, what was the title he was actually looking for? Because I misheard him also.
—“Poor Carmine”. Oh, I thought he said, “Porcupine”. [laughs]
—Okay. Lemme jump in here. This guy asked me—
—for “Porcupine”. He asked Max back for “Porcupine”. So I don’t know what this dude is doing, but he’s pullin’ some bullshit. Oh, god, I don’t know—
Thanks for holding. Um, I’m sort of coming up a little empty-handed, here. Do you—
Oh, weird! Okay, so on the first pass I heard “Porcupine”, which is—
—That’s weird. I put somebody on hold and then when I picked up, you were there.
[gasps] Oh, my goodness! Someone just called us!
No, I think it’s called “Wine & Dine”.
Weird. Okay, I’m gonna hang up. I think this is on you; we’re a used book store, so I’m guessing it’s not for us. Anyway—
—I don’t know. We’re a new and used book store.
Oh! Well, we wouldn’t have—they said that they were looking for multiple copies.
Weird. So weird. Scam, I guess. I don’t know—
[laughing] It—it—I hope that you have success with this! [chuckles]
Hi, okay! So I am not finding anything called “Porcupine”. Are you sure it was this store—this specific Barbara’s?
Ah! This is not Barbara’s; this is Volumes.
Oh! Oh, I’m sorr—what? I was just on the phone with a customer!
They just called me and were looking for a book.
Wait! And how are we talking, though? That doesn’t make sense! [snickers]—
—Um, okay! Oh okay, that’s really, really weird. Well! Maybe—
They were looking for a book called “Porcupine”.
Ah! See, mine was looking for “Pickel Vine”!
—This is a glitch in the matrix! I don’t know, but, um…
Alright, it looks like there’s a few books called “Porcupine”. Um—
—Did you just—are we conn—is this another store I’m talking to?
This is the Hirshhorn location. But I have access to the inventory of other stores.
O—okay. This is Book—sorry, I—this is—this is Book and Music Exchange, here—I’m from Book and Music Exchange. This guy just put us together on a call looking for a book called “Snorting Lines”.
Okay, I’m not—yeah, I’m not seeing that.
Yeah, I—that’s—that’s what I’m—it’s—it’s okay. We’re okay—
—Have a nice day! [sneers to self] Yay, Customer Service!
Are you looking for, like “porcupine”, like the animal?
Oh, hey! I was talking to someone about a book.
Yeah! I was also talking to someone about a book.
[snickers] Oh, I was talking to a different person; it was a—
I was talking to a man! Okay! This is very strange!. Do you also work at a book shop?
—I’m working at a book shop. This is very strange!

—what are you looking for? I was also helping a man, and he was like, “I’m looking for a book called Porcupine towards the right of your store.” And I was like, I have no idea what you’re talking about—
Oh! He—okay, so he was asking both of us, from different book stores?—
—I guess so! This is so weird!—
—[laughs] Wow. I wonder if he just pranked us [laughs]—
—This is so weird! I have no idea how this happened!—
[voicemail message of Queen Anne Book Company plays quietly]
—Thank you for calling the Queen Anne Book Company; we’re located at the top of Queen Anne Hill—
—[laughs] Okay, well, I don’t think that you and I can probably help each other!—
—This is weird! I hope you have a wonderful day! Thank you so much! God bless!—
—our holiday hours are 10 AM to 6 PM daily, 7 days a week—
